title: Listen and Choose a Response — Campus Life

questions.stem: Excuse me, do you know when the library opens on weekends?

options.A: Yes, it opens at 10 AM on Saturdays and Sundays.
options.B: The library is closed for renovations.
options.C: I usually study at the coffee shop.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for specific information about weekend hours, so a direct answer about opening time is the most appropriate response.

questions.stem: I'm having trouble with this math problem. Could you take a look?

options.A: Sure, let me see what I can do.
options.B: Math is my favorite subject too.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for help, so a willing offer to assist is the expected response.

questions.stem: I'm starving, but I don't know what to eat around here.

options.A: You should have brought lunch today.
options.B: There's a great ramen place just across the street.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing hunger and indecision, so a helpful suggestion of where to eat is the most natural response.

questions.stem: You've been studying for hours. Don't you think you should take a break?

options.A: No, I'm almost done with this chapter.
options.B: I didn't go to the library yesterday.
options.C: The exam is next Monday, right?

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing concern about overworking, so acknowledging the concern while explaining your plan is the most natural response.

questions.stem: We're still on for the group presentation tomorrow, right?

options.A: I presented my project last week.
options.B: Yes, I'll meet you in the conference room at 2 PM.
options.C: The group project is worth 30% of our grade.

questions.explanation: The speaker is confirming existing plans, so an affirmative response with specific details about the meeting is appropriate.

questions.stem: I'm really sorry I forgot to bring your textbook. I'll bring it tomorrow.

options.A: You should be more careful with library books.
options.B: That's okay, I didn't need it for today's class anyway.

questions.explanation: The speaker is apologizing, so a gracious acceptance of the apology and reassurance is the most appropriate response.

questions.stem: Would it be alright if I submitted my paper a day late? I've been sick.

options.A: You should see a doctor first.
options.B: The deadline was already extended once.
options.C: Sure, just make sure to get it in by Wednesday.

questions.explanation: The speaker is politely requesting an extension, so a gracious approval with a clear new deadline is the appropriate response from a professor.

questions.stem: Wait, you mean the campus tour starts at 8 AM, not 9?

options.A: Yes, you'll receive a campus map at the tour.
options.B: That's right. The schedule was updated yesterday.
options.C: There are several tours available throughout the week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing surprise about a time change and seeking confirmation, so confirming the updated information is most appropriate.

audioSegments.text: Excuse me, do you know when the library opens on weekends?
I'm having trouble with this math problem. Could you take a look?
I'm starving, but I don't know what to eat around here.
You've been studying for hours. Don't you think you should take a break?
We're still on for the group presentation tomorrow, right?
I'm really sorry I forgot to bring your textbook. I'll bring it tomorrow.
Would it be alright if I submitted my paper a day late? I've been sick.
Wait, you mean the campus tour starts at 8 AM, not 9?
